revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun in southern Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Rōji Kofun | native_name =老司古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Rōji Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|31|49|N|130|25|12|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Minami-ku, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|31|49|N|130|25|12|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Rōji Kofun'''|老司古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in [[Minami-ku, Fukuoka]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2000.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3272|title=老司古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Rōji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is situated on a hill at an elevation of approximately 40 meters on the west bank of the Naka River, orientated to the south. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out by [[Kyushu University]] and the Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education from 1965 to 1969, and by the Fukuoka City Board of Education in 1987. The tumulus measures 76 meters in total length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion and 45-meter anterior rectangular width. The mound had ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. Three stone [[burial chamber]]s have been confirmed, with two in the posterior portion and one in the anterior portion. Burial chamber No. 1 in the center of the rear circle is the oldest, measuring 2.1 meters in length, 95-cm in width, and 80-cm in height.There is a corridor leading to the front part, and ten [[bronze mirror]]s including a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror, were found as [[grave goods]]. From these artifacts, it estimated that the tumulus was built around the beginning of the 5th century. Other grave goods included cylindrical beads and ''[[magatama]]'', weapons such as iron swords and iron arrowheads, short armor, [[horse harness]]es, and tools. From the human bones surviving inside the burial chamber, it is known that multiple adult men and women were buried there..<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located on the grounds of Fukuoka Juvenile Detention Center and is normally not accessible to the public.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/437 Fukuoka City home page]{{in lang|ja}}

Burial chamber No. 1 in the center of the rear circle is the oldest, measuring 2.1 meters in length, 95-cm in width, and 80-cm in height.There is a corridor leading to the front part, and ten [[bronze mirror]]s including a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror, were found as [[grave goods]]. From these artifacts, it estimated that the tumulus was built around the beginning of the 5th century. Other grave goods included cylindrical beads and ''[[magatama]]'', weapons such as iron swords and iron arrowheads, short armor, [[horse harness]]es, and tools. From the human bones surviving inside the burial chamber, it is known that multiple adult men and women were buried there..<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{Short description|Kofun in southern Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Rōji Kofun | native_name =老司古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Rōji Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|31|49|N|130|25|12|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Minami-ku, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|31|49|N|130|25|12|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Rōji Kofun'''|老司古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in [[Minami-ku, Fukuoka]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2000.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3272|title=老司古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Rōji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is situated on a hill at an elevation of approximately 40 meters on the west bank of the Naka River, orientated to the south. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out by [[Kyushu University]] and the Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education from 1965 to 1969, and by the Fukuoka City Board of Education in 1987. The tumulus measures 76 meters in total length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion and 45-meter anterior rectangular width. The mound had ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. Three stone [[burial chamber]]s have been confirmed, with two in the posterior portion and one in the anterior portion. Burial chamber No. 1 in the center of the rear circle is the oldest, measuring 2.1 meters in length, 95-cm in width, and 80-cm in height.There is a corridor leading to the front part, and ten [[bronze mirror]]s including a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror, were found as [[grave goods]]. From these artifacts, it estimated that the tumulus was built around the beginning of the 5th century. Other grave goods included cylindrical beads and ''[[magatama]]'', weapons such as iron swords and iron arrowheads, short armor, [[horse harness]]es, and tools. From the human bones surviving inside the burial chamber, it is known that multiple adult men and women were buried there..<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located on the grounds of Fukuoka Juvenile Detention Center and is normally not accessible to the public.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun in southern Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Rōji Kofun | native_name =老司古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Rōji Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|31|49|N|130|25|12|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Minami-ku, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|31|49|N|130|25|12|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Rōji Kofun'''|老司古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in [[Minami-ku, Fukuoka]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2000.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3272|title=老司古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Rōji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is situated on a hill at an elevation of approximately 40 meters on the west bank of the Naka River, orientated to the south. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out by [[Kyushu University]] and the Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education from 1965 to 1969, and by the Fukuoka City Board of Education in 1987. The tumulus measures 76 meters in total length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion and 45-meter anterior rectangular width. The mound had ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. Three stone [[burial chamber]]s have been confirmed, with two in the posterior portion and one in the anterior portion. Burial chamber No. 1 in the center of the rear circle is the oldest, measuring 2.1 meters in length, 95-cm in width, and 80-cm in height.There is a corridor leading to the front part, and ten [[bronze mirror]]s including a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror, were found as [[grave goods]]. From these artifacts, it estimated that the tumulus was built around the beginning of the 5th century. Other grave goods included cylindrical beads and ''[[magatama]]'', weapons such as iron swords and iron arrowheads, short armor, [[horse harness]]es, and tools. From the human bones surviving inside the burial chamber, it is known that multiple adult men and women were buried there..<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located on the grounds of Fukuoka Juvenile Detention Center and is normally not accessible to the public.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/437 Fukuoka City home page]{{in lang|ja}}

The tumulus measures 76 meters in total length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion and 45-meter anterior rectangular width. The mound had ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. Three stone [[burial chamber]]s have been confirmed, with two in the posterior portion and one in the anterior portion. Burial chamber No. 1 in the center of the rear circle is the oldest, measuring 2.1 meters in length, 95-cm in width, and 80-cm in height.There is a corridor leading to the front part, and ten [[bronze mirror]]s including a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror, were found as [[grave goods]]. From these artifacts, it estimated that the tumulus was built around the beginning of the 5th century. Other grave goods included cylindrical beads and ''[[magatama]]'', weapons such as iron swords and iron arrowheads, short armor, [[horse harness]]es, and tools. From the human bones surviving inside the burial chamber, it is known that multiple adult men and women were buried there..<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located on the grounds of Fukuoka Juvenile Detention Center and is normally not accessible to the public.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]

The {{nihongo|'''Rōji Kofun'''|老司古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in [[Minami-ku, Fukuoka]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2000.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3272|title=老司古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Rōji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is situated on a hill at an elevation of approximately 40 meters on the west bank of the Naka River, orientated to the south. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out by [[Kyushu University]] and the Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education from 1965 to 1969, and by the Fukuoka City Board of Education in 1987. The tumulus measures 76 meters in total length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion and 45-meter anterior rectangular width. The mound had ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. Three stone [[burial chamber]]s have been confirmed, with two in the posterior portion and one in the anterior portion. Burial chamber No. 1 in the center of the rear circle is the oldest, measuring 2.1 meters in length, 95-cm in width, and 80-cm in height.There is a corridor leading to the front part, and ten [[bronze mirror]]s including a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror, were found as [[grave goods]]. From these artifacts, it estimated that the tumulus was built around the beginning of the 5th century. Other grave goods included cylindrical beads and ''[[magatama]]'', weapons such as iron swords and iron arrowheads, short armor, [[horse harness]]es, and tools. From the human bones surviving inside the burial chamber, it is known that multiple adult men and women were buried there..<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located on the grounds of Fukuoka Juvenile Detention Center and is normally not accessible to the public.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Rōji Kofun'''|老司古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in [[:en:Minami-ku, Fukuoka|Minami-ku, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2000.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3272|title=老司古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Rōji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is situated on a hill at an elevation of approximately 40 meters on the west bank of the Naka River, orientated to the south. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out by [[:en:Kyushu University|Kyushu University]] and the Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education from 1965 to 1969, and by the Fukuoka City Board of Education in 1987. The tumulus measures 76 meters in total length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion and 45-meter anterior rectangular width. The mound had ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. Three stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s have been confirmed, with two in the posterior portion and one in the anterior portion. Burial chamber No. 1 in the center of the rear circle is the oldest, measuring 2.1 meters in length, 95-cm in width, and 80-cm in height.There is a corridor leading to the front part, and ten [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s including a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror, were found as [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. From these artifacts, it estimated that the tumulus was built around the beginning of the 5th century. Other grave goods included cylindrical beads and ''[[magatama]]'', weapons such as iron swords and iron arrowheads, short armor, [[:en:Horse harness|Horse harness]]es, and tools. From the human bones surviving inside the burial chamber, it is known that multiple adult men and women were buried there..<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located on the grounds of Fukuoka Juvenile Detention Center and is normally not accessible to the public.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/437 Fukuoka City home page]{{in lang|ja}}

From the human bones surviving inside the burial chamber, it is known that multiple adult men and women were buried there..<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located on the grounds of Fukuoka Juvenile Detention Center and is normally not accessible to the public.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Rōji Kofun'''|老司古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in [[:en:Minami-ku, Fukuoka|Minami-ku, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2000.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3272|title=老司古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Rōji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is situated on a hill at an elevation of approximately 40 meters on the west bank of the Naka River, orientated to the south. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out by [[:en:Kyushu University|Kyushu University]] and the Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education from 1965 to 1969, and by the Fukuoka City Board of Education in 1987. The tumulus measures 76 meters in total length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion and 45-meter anterior rectangular width. The mound had ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. Three stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s have been confirmed, with two in the posterior portion and one in the anterior portion. Burial chamber No. 1 in the center of the rear circle is the oldest, measuring 2.1 meters in length, 95-cm in width, and 80-cm in height.There is a corridor leading to the front part, and ten [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s including a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror, were found as [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. From these artifacts, it estimated that the tumulus was built around the beginning of the 5th century. Other grave goods included cylindrical beads and ''[[magatama]]'', weapons such as iron swords and iron arrowheads, short armor, [[:en:Horse harness|Horse harness]]es, and tools. From the human bones surviving inside the burial chamber, it is known that multiple adult men and women were buried there..<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located on the grounds of Fukuoka Juvenile Detention Center and is normally not accessible to the public.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2000.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3272|title=老司古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Rōji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is situated on a hill at an elevation of approximately 40 meters on the west bank of the Naka River, orientated to the south. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out by [[:en:Kyushu University|Kyushu University]] and the Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education from 1965 to 1969, and by the Fukuoka City Board of Education in 1987. The tumulus measures 76 meters in total length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion and 45-meter anterior rectangular width. The mound had ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. Three stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s have been confirmed, with two in the posterior portion and one in the anterior portion. Burial chamber No. 1 in the center of the rear circle is the oldest, measuring 2.1 meters in length, 95-cm in width, and 80-cm in height.There is a corridor leading to the front part, and ten [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s including a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror, were found as [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. From these artifacts, it estimated that the tumulus was built around the beginning of the 5th century. Other grave goods included cylindrical beads and ''[[magatama]]'', weapons such as iron swords and iron arrowheads, short armor, [[:en:Horse harness|Horse harness]]es, and tools. From the human bones surviving inside the burial chamber, it is known that multiple adult men and women were buried there..<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located on the grounds of Fukuoka Juvenile Detention Center and is normally not accessible to the public.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Rōji Kofun]][[ja:老司古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Rōji Kofun]][[ja:老司古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Rōji Kofun]][[ja:老司古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18459458]] Wikidata name: Rōji Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun in southern Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Rōji Kofun | native_name =老司古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Rōji Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.530444|N|130.42025|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Minami-ku, Fukuoka|Minami-ku, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|31|49|N|130|25|12|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Rōji Kofun'''|老司古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in [[:en:Minami-ku, Fukuoka|Minami-ku, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2000.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3272|title=老司古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Rōji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is situated on a hill at an elevation of approximately 40 meters on the west bank of the Naka River, orientated to the south. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out by [[:en:Kyushu University|Kyushu University]] and the Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education from 1965 to 1969, and by the Fukuoka City Board of Education in 1987. The tumulus measures 76 meters in total length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion and 45-meter anterior rectangular width. The mound had ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. Three stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s have been confirmed, with two in the posterior portion and one in the anterior portion. Burial chamber No. 1 in the center of the rear circle is the oldest, measuring 2.1 meters in length, 95-cm in width, and 80-cm in height.There is a corridor leading to the front part, and ten [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s including a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror, were found as [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. From these artifacts, it estimated that the tumulus was built around the beginning of the 5th century. Other grave goods included cylindrical beads and ''[[magatama]]'', weapons such as iron swords and iron arrowheads, short armor, [[:en:Horse harness|Horse harness]]es, and tools. From the human bones surviving inside the burial chamber, it is known that multiple adult men and women were buried there..<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located on the grounds of Fukuoka Juvenile Detention Center and is normally not accessible to the public.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/437 Fukuoka City home page]{{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Rōji Kofun'''|老司古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in {{ill|Minami-ku, Fukuoka|en|Minami-ku, Fukuoka|WD=Q1132419|lt=Minami-ku, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2000.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3272|title=老司古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Rōji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is situated on a hill at an elevation of approximately 40 meters on the west bank of the Naka River, orientated to the south. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out by {{ill|Kyushu University|en|Kyushu University|WD=Q1188786|lt=Kyushu University}} and the Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education from 1965 to 1969, and by the Fukuoka City Board of Education in 1987. The tumulus measures 76 meters in total length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion and 45-meter anterior rectangular width. The mound had ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}''. Three stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s have been confirmed, with two in the posterior portion and one in the anterior portion. Burial chamber No. 1 in the center of the rear circle is the oldest, measuring 2.1 meters in length, 95-cm in width, and 80-cm in height.There is a corridor leading to the front part, and ten {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s including a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror, were found as {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}}. From these artifacts, it estimated that the tumulus was built around the beginning of the 5th century. Other grave goods included cylindrical beads and ''[[magatama]]'', weapons such as iron swords and iron arrowheads, short armor, {{ill|Horse harness|en|Horse harness|WD=Q3127570|lt=Horse harness}}es, and tools. From the human bones surviving inside the burial chamber, it is known that multiple adult men and women were buried there..<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located on the grounds of Fukuoka Juvenile Detention Center and is normally not accessible to the public.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/437 Fukuoka City home page]{{in lang|ja}}

The tumulus measures 76 meters in total length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion and 45-meter anterior rectangular width. The mound had ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}''. Three stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s have been confirmed, with two in the posterior portion and one in the anterior portion. Burial chamber No. 1 in the center of the rear circle is the oldest, measuring 2.1 meters in length, 95-cm in width, and 80-cm in height.There is a corridor leading to the front part, and ten {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s including a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror, were found as {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}}. From these artifacts, it estimated that the tumulus was built around the beginning of the 5th century. Other grave goods included cylindrical beads and ''[[magatama]]'', weapons such as iron swords and iron arrowheads, short armor, {{ill|Horse harness|en|Horse harness|WD=Q3127570|lt=Horse harness}}es, and tools. From the human bones surviving inside the burial chamber, it is known that multiple adult men and women were buried there..<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located on the grounds of Fukuoka Juvenile Detention Center and is normally not accessible to the public.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

From the human bones surviving inside the burial chamber, it is known that multiple adult men and women were buried there..<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Rōji Kofun]][[ja:老司古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Rōji Kofun]][[ja:老司古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Rōji Kofun]][[ja:老司古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun in southern Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Rōji Kofun | native_name =老司古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Rōji Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.530444|N|130.42025|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Minami-ku, Fukuoka|en|Minami-ku, Fukuoka|WD=Q1132419|lt=Minami-ku, Fukuoka|1=Minami-ku, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|31|49|N|130|25|12|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Rōji Kofun'''|老司古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in {{ill|Minami-ku, Fukuoka|en|Minami-ku, Fukuoka|WD=Q1132419|lt=Minami-ku, Fukuoka|1=Minami-ku, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2000.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3272|title=老司古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Rōji Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is situated on a hill at an elevation of approximately 40 meters on the west bank of the Naka River, orientated to the south. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out by {{ill|Kyushu University|en|Kyushu University|WD=Q1188786|lt=Kyushu University|1=Kyushu University}} and the Fukuoka Prefectural Board of Education from 1965 to 1969, and by the Fukuoka City Board of Education in 1987. The tumulus measures 76 meters in total length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion and 45-meter anterior rectangular width. The mound had ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}''. Three stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s have been confirmed, with two in the posterior portion and one in the anterior portion. Burial chamber No. 1 in the center of the rear circle is the oldest, measuring 2.1 meters in length, 95-cm in width, and 80-cm in height.There is a corridor leading to the front part, and ten {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s including a triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror, were found as {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}. From these artifacts, it estimated that the tumulus was built around the beginning of the 5th century. Other grave goods included cylindrical beads and ''[[magatama]]'', weapons such as iron swords and iron arrowheads, short armor, {{ill|Horse harness|en|Horse harness|WD=Q3127570|lt=Horse harness|1=Horse harness}}es, and tools. From the human bones surviving inside the burial chamber, it is known that multiple adult men and women were buried there..<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is located on the grounds of Fukuoka Juvenile Detention Center and is normally not accessible to the public.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/437 Fukuoka City home page]{{in lang|ja}}
